name: Linguine a La Anne
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the linguine according to package directions and drain.
2. Return the cooked linguine to the pot used for boiling.
3. Melt 4 tablespoons of butter in a saucepan.
4. Blend flour and salt, then add the liquid from the mushrooms, water, evaporated milk, and chicken bouillon cube.
5. Bring to a boil and stir until the sauce thickens.
6. Add 2 cups of the sauce and the drained mushrooms to the linguine and toss until well mixed.
7. Spoon the linguine mixture into a 13x9 inch baking dish, pressing up the sides to leave a slight hollow in the center.
8. Toss the ham in the remaining sauce and spoon it into the hollow in the pasta.
9. Sprinkle with Romano cheese and refrigerate until chilled.
10. When ready to serve, bake at 400°F for 20 minutes.
11. Meanwhile, sauté sliced peppers in the remaining butter until soft.
12. Serve with sautéed red and green peppers around the edge of the casserole and croutons sprinkled on top.
